message:
refactor(notify): unify on upstream-changed dispatch event with flat payload

Replace tool-specific dispatch events (installer-updated, etc.) with a
single canonical `upstream-changed` event type. The receiver maps to
legacy event types internally via case statement for backward compat.

Sender templates:
- Send event-type: upstream-changed with flat payload (tool, source_repo,
  ref, sha, actor, timestamp) instead of nested structures with checksums
- Introduce explicit TOOL_NAME env var (ACFS checksums.yaml key doesn't
  always match GitHub repo name)
- Standardize secret name to ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN
- Narrow branch triggers from [main, master] to [main] only

Receiver workflow:
- Parse entire client_payload as JSON blob via jq with fallback paths
  (.source_repo // .repo // .commit.repo // empty)
- Default missing SOURCE_REPO/SOURCE_COMMIT to "unknown" instead of failing
- Accept upstream-changed in types list alongside legacy event types

Docs and E2E tests updated to match new payload shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@ name: Installer Notification Receiver
 
 on:
   repository_dispatch:
-    types: [installer-updated, installer-removed, installer-added]
+    types: [installer-updated, installer-removed, installer-added, upstream-changed]
   workflow_dispatch:
     inputs:
       tool_name:
@@ -60,13 +60,8 @@ jobs:
         id: validate
         env:
           INPUT_TOOL: ${{ github.event.inputs.tool_name }}
-          PAYLOAD_TOOL: ${{ github.event.client_payload.tool }}
+          PAYLOAD_JSON: ${{ toJson(github.event.client_payload) }}
           PAYLOAD_ACTION: ${{ github.event.action }}
-          PAYLOAD_NEW_SHA256: ${{ github.event.client_payload.new_sha256 }}
-          PAYLOAD_OLD_SHA256: ${{ github.event.client_payload.old_sha256 }}
-          PAYLOAD_REPO: ${{ github.event.client_payload.repo }}
-          PAYLOAD_COMMIT: ${{ github.event.client_payload.commit }}
-          PAYLOAD_URL: ${{ github.event.client_payload.url }}
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
 
@@ -80,14 +75,27 @@ jobs:
             OLD_SHA256=""
             SOURCE_REPO="manual"
             SOURCE_COMMIT="manual"
+            INSTALLER_URL=""
           else
             # Extract from repository_dispatch payload
-            TOOL_NAME="$PAYLOAD_TOOL"
-            EVENT_TYPE="$PAYLOAD_ACTION"
-            NEW_SHA256="$PAYLOAD_NEW_SHA256"
-            OLD_SHA256="$PAYLOAD_OLD_SHA256"
-            SOURCE_REPO="$PAYLOAD_REPO"
-            SOURCE_COMMIT="$PAYLOAD_COMMIT"
+            TOOL_NAME="$(jq -r '.tool // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
+            RAW_EVENT_TYPE="$PAYLOAD_ACTION"
+            case "$RAW_EVENT_TYPE" in
+              upstream-changed)
+                EVENT_TYPE="installer-updated"
+                ;;
+              installer-updated|installer-removed|installer-added)
+                EVENT_TYPE="$RAW_EVENT_TYPE"
+                ;;
+              *)
+                EVENT_TYPE="$RAW_EVENT_TYPE"
+                ;;
+            esac
+            NEW_SHA256="$(jq -r '.new_sha256 // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
+            OLD_SHA256="$(jq -r '.old_sha256 // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
+            SOURCE_REPO="$(jq -r '.source_repo // .repo // .commit.repo // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
+            SOURCE_COMMIT="$(jq -r '.sha // .commit.sha // .commit // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
+            INSTALLER_URL="$(jq -r '.url // empty' <<< "$PAYLOAD_JSON")"
           fi
 
           echo "Tool: $TOOL_NAME"
@@ -110,17 +118,20 @@ jobs:
 
           # Validate dispatch provenance fields when event came from repository_dispatch
           if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" != "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
-            if ! [[ "$SOURCE_REPO" =~ ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ ]]; then
+            if [[ -z "$SOURCE_REPO" ]]; then
+              SOURCE_REPO="unknown"
+            elif ! [[ "$SOURCE_REPO" =~ ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ ]]; then
               echo "::error::Invalid source repository format: $SOURCE_REPO"
               echo "is_valid=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
               exit 1
             fi
-            if [[ -n "$SOURCE_COMMIT" ]] && ! [[ "$SOURCE_COMMIT" =~ ^[0-9a-fA-F]{7,40}$ ]]; then
+            if [[ -z "$SOURCE_COMMIT" ]]; then
+              SOURCE_COMMIT="unknown"
+            elif ! [[ "$SOURCE_COMMIT" =~ ^[0-9a-fA-F]{7,40}$ ]]; then
               echo "::error::Invalid source commit SHA: $SOURCE_COMMIT"
               echo "is_valid=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
               exit 1
             fi
-            SOURCE_COMMIT="${SOURCE_COMMIT:-unknown}"
           fi
 
           # Check if tool exists in checksums.yaml (for update/remove)
@@ -134,7 +145,7 @@ jobs:
 
           # Get URL from checksums.yaml (or from payload for new tools)
           if [[ "$EVENT_TYPE" == "installer-added" ]]; then
-            INSTALLER_URL="$PAYLOAD_URL"
+            :
           else
             INSTALLER_URL=$(yq e ".installers[\"$TOOL_NAME\"].url // \"\"" "$CHECKSUMS_FILE")
           fi

@@ -71,14 +71,18 @@ name: Notify ACFS of Installer Changes
 
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       # Adjust this path to match your install script location
       - 'install.sh'
       # For repos with scripts/install.sh:
       # - 'scripts/install.sh'
   workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger for testing
 
+env:
+  # IMPORTANT: this must match the installer key in ACFS checksums.yaml.
+  TOOL_NAME: "replace-with-acfs-tool-key"
+
 jobs:
   notify-acfs:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
@@ -91,7 +95,8 @@ jobs:
           event-type: upstream-changed
           client-payload: |
             {
-              "tool": "${{ github.event.repository.name }}",
+              "tool": "${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}",
+              "source_repo": "${{ github.repository }}",
               "ref": "${{ github.ref }}",
               "sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
               "actor": "${{ github.actor }}",
@@ -101,11 +106,13 @@ jobs:
       - name: Log dispatch
         run: |
           echo "✅ Dispatched upstream-changed event to ACFS"
-          echo "   Tool: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
+          echo "   Tool: ${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}"
           echo "   SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"
           echo "   Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"
 ```
 
+`TOOL_NAME` must match the key under `installers:` in ACFS `checksums.yaml`. Do not assume the GitHub repository name matches the ACFS tool key.
+
 ## Tool-Specific Path Configuration
 
 Different tools have different installer locations. Adjust the `paths` trigger accordingly:
@@ -132,7 +139,7 @@ Different tools have different installer locations. Adjust the `paths` trigger a
 ```yaml
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'install.sh'
 ```
@@ -142,7 +149,7 @@ on:
 ```yaml
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'scripts/install.sh'
 ```
@@ -152,7 +159,7 @@ on:
 ```yaml
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'install.sh'
       - 'scripts/*.sh'

@@ -7,18 +7,23 @@
 #   1. Create a PAT with 'contents:read' on agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
 #   2. Add it as a secret named ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN in this repo
 #   3. Copy this file to .github/workflows/notify-acfs.yml
+#   4. Set TOOL_NAME to the matching key in ACFS checksums.yaml
 #
 # Related: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b04c
 #
 name: Notify ACFS of Installer Changes
 
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'install.sh'
   workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger for testing
 
+env:
+  # REQUIRED: Update this to match the installer key in ACFS checksums.yaml.
+  TOOL_NAME: '{{ TOOL_NAME_PLACEHOLDER }}'
+
 jobs:
   notify-acfs:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
@@ -31,7 +36,8 @@ jobs:
           event-type: upstream-changed
           client-payload: |
             {
-              "tool": "${{ github.event.repository.name }}",
+              "tool": "${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}",
+              "source_repo": "${{ github.repository }}",
               "ref": "${{ github.ref }}",
               "sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
               "actor": "${{ github.actor }}",
@@ -41,6 +47,6 @@ jobs:
       - name: Log dispatch
         run: |
           echo "✅ Dispatched upstream-changed event to ACFS"
-          echo "   Tool: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
+          echo "   Tool: ${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}"
           echo "   SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"
           echo "   Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

@@ -7,18 +7,23 @@
 #   1. Create a PAT with 'contents:read' on agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
 #   2. Add it as a secret named ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN in this repo
 #   3. Copy this file to .github/workflows/notify-acfs.yml
+#   4. Set TOOL_NAME to the matching key in ACFS checksums.yaml
 #
 # Related: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b04c
 #
 name: Notify ACFS of Installer Changes
 
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'scripts/install.sh'
   workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger for testing
 
+env:
+  # REQUIRED: Update this to match the installer key in ACFS checksums.yaml.
+  TOOL_NAME: '{{ TOOL_NAME_PLACEHOLDER }}'
+
 jobs:
   notify-acfs:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
@@ -31,7 +36,8 @@ jobs:
           event-type: upstream-changed
           client-payload: |
             {
-              "tool": "${{ github.event.repository.name }}",
+              "tool": "${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}",
+              "source_repo": "${{ github.repository }}",
               "ref": "${{ github.ref }}",
               "sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
               "actor": "${{ github.actor }}",
@@ -41,6 +47,6 @@ jobs:
       - name: Log dispatch
         run: |
           echo "✅ Dispatched upstream-changed event to ACFS"
-          echo "   Tool: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
+          echo "   Tool: ${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}"
           echo "   SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"
           echo "   Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

@@ -2,8 +2,9 @@
 # ============================================================
 # E2E Test: Full Notification Chain Verification
 #
-# Tests the GitHub Actions workflow that receives installer update
-# notifications and creates PRs for checksum updates.
+# Tests the GitHub Actions workflow chain for installer change notifications.
+# The canonical fast path is `upstream-changed`; legacy receiver-specific
+# events remain covered for compatibility.
 #
 # Related: bead bd-19y9.2.5
 #
@@ -154,9 +155,9 @@ test_1_matching_checksum() {
 
     log "INFO" "Current zoxide checksum: ${current_sha:0:16}..."
 
-    # Send dispatch with matching checksum
-    local payload="{\"tool\":\"zoxide\",\"new_sha256\":\"$current_sha\",\"old_sha256\":\"$current_sha\",\"repo\":\"test\",\"commit\":\"test123\"}"
-    send_dispatch "installer-updated" "$payload"
+    # Send canonical upstream-changed event for an installer that should already match.
+    local payload="{\"tool\":\"zoxide\",\"source_repo\":\"ajeetdsouza/zoxide\",\"sha\":\"test1234\",\"ref\":\"refs/heads/main\",\"actor\":\"e2e-test\",\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\"}"
+    send_dispatch "upstream-changed" "$payload"
 
     # Check workflow result
     if wait_for_workflow "success" 60; then
@@ -194,7 +195,7 @@ test_3_malformed_payload() {
 
     # Send malformed payload (missing required fields)
     local payload="{\"bad_field\":\"value\"}"
-    send_dispatch "installer-updated" "$payload" || true
+    send_dispatch "upstream-changed" "$payload" || true
 
     sleep 20
 
@@ -300,7 +301,7 @@ test_7_workflow_dispatches_configured() {
 
         # Check for expected event types
         local events_found=0
-        for event in "installer-updated" "installer-removed" "installer-added"; do
+        for event in "upstream-changed" "installer-updated" "installer-removed" "installer-added"; do
             if grep -q "$event" "$workflow_file"; then
                 log "INFO" "  Found event type: $event"
                 ((events_found++)) || true

@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
 #
 # Setup:
 # 1. Copy this file to .github/workflows/notify-acfs.yml
-# 2. Create a repository secret ACFS_NOTIFY_TOKEN with a GitHub PAT
+# 2. Create a repository secret ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN with a GitHub PAT
 #    that has repo scope for Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
 # 3. Update TOOL_NAME to match the entry in ACFS checksums.yaml
 #
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ name: Notify ACFS of Installer Changes
 
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [main, master]
+    branches: [main]
     paths:
       - 'install.sh'
       - 'scripts/install.sh'
@@ -37,7 +37,8 @@ on:
         type: boolean
 
 env:
-  # REQUIRED: Update this to match your entry in ACFS checksums.yaml
+  # REQUIRED: Update this to match the installer key in ACFS checksums.yaml.
+  # Do not assume the repo name matches the checksum entry.
   TOOL_NAME: '{{ TOOL_NAME_PLACEHOLDER }}'
 
   # Path to installer script (update if different)
@@ -107,33 +108,18 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
             --arg tool "${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}" \
-            --arg new_sha256 "${{ steps.checksum.outputs.sha256 }}" \
-            --arg old_sha256 "${{ steps.previous.outputs.prev_sha256 }}" \
-            --arg commit_sha "${{ github.sha }}" \
-            --arg commit_message "${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}" \
-            --arg repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
-            --arg branch "${{ github.ref_name }}" \
+            --arg source_repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
+            --arg ref "${{ github.ref }}" \
+            --arg sha "${{ github.sha }}" \
             --arg actor "${{ github.actor }}" \
-            --arg installer_path "${{ env.INSTALLER_PATH }}" \
-            --arg installer_size "${{ steps.validate.outputs.installer_size }}" \
-            --arg run_id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
+            --arg timestamp "$(date -Iseconds)" \
             '{
               tool: $tool,
-              new_sha256: $new_sha256,
-              old_sha256: $old_sha256,
-              commit: {
-                sha: $commit_sha,
-                message: ($commit_message | split("\n")[0]),
-                repo: $repo,
-                branch: $branch,
-                actor: $actor
-              },
-              installer: {
-                path: $installer_path,
-                size_bytes: ($installer_size | tonumber)
-              },
-              workflow_run_id: $run_id,
-              timestamp: (now | todate)
+              source_repo: $source_repo,
+              ref: $ref,
+              sha: $sha,
+              actor: $actor,
+              timestamp: $timestamp
             }')
           echo "payload<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           echo "$PAYLOAD" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
@@ -147,7 +133,7 @@ jobs:
           github.event.inputs.dry_run == 'true'
         run: |
           echo "::notice::DRY RUN - Would send notification to ACFS"
-          echo "Event type: installer-updated"
+          echo "Event type: upstream-changed"
           echo "Payload:"
           echo '${{ steps.payload.outputs.payload }}' | jq .
 
@@ -157,9 +143,9 @@ jobs:
           github.event.inputs.dry_run != 'true'
         uses: peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3
         with:
-          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_NOTIFY_TOKEN }}
+          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}
           repository: ${{ env.ACFS_REPO }}
-          event-type: installer-updated
+          event-type: upstream-changed
           client-payload: ${{ steps.payload.outputs.payload }}
 
       - name: Log result
@@ -189,13 +175,13 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - uses: peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3
         with:
-          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_NOTIFY_TOKEN }}
+          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}
           repository: Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
           event-type: installer-removed
           client-payload: |
             {
               "tool": "${{ env.TOOL_NAME }}",
-              "commit_sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
-              "repo": "${{ github.repository }}",
+              "source_repo": "${{ github.repository }}",
+              "sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
               "reason": "Installer deprecated or removed"
             }
